description:
You are acting as a DBA who has been asked to quickly check whether the
current query plan for reading the whole “orders” table is still using a
sequential scan.  
A single container called “pg-optimize” (running a PostgreSQL 15
instance) is already up and running under your user account.  
Inside that container there is a database named “shop” and a table named
“orders”.

Goal
-----
1. Run exactly one *interactive* terminal command (no multi-line shell
   scripts, no chaining with “&&” or “;”) that:
   • connects to the running container,  
   • runs an EXPLAIN on the given query, and  
   • redirects the complete textual output to a log file on the host.  
2. Save the EXPLAIN output to a host-side file
   “/home/user/query_plans/orders_full_scan.log”.
3. Do **not** modify anything else on disk.

Strict output format
--------------------
The automated grader will read the log file verbatim.  Therefore:

• The first non-empty line **must** be the string
  “QUERY PLAN”.  
• The second line **must** consist only of 11 hyphens
  (“-----------”).  
• At least one subsequent line **must** contain the substring
  “Seq Scan on orders”.

No extra lines or leading/trailing spaces are permitted before “QUERY
PLAN”.  A terminating newline at the very end of the file **is**
required.

Hints
-----
• A typical psql invocation already prints the required header lines in
  that exact casing, so you do not need to alter the output.  
• Remember to supply the database name (“shop”) and user (“postgres”)
  when calling psql inside the container.  
• You do not have root privileges; the container is owned by your user,
  so ordinary `docker` / `podman` commands will work.  
• The directory “/home/user/query_plans” already exists and is writable
  by you.

After you have run the single command, verify that the file
“/home/user/query_plans/orders_full_scan.log” exists and exactly follows
the format above.  Once satisfied, you are done.
truth:
Pre-existing resources
----------------------
1. Host directory:  /home/user/query_plans  (mode 755, owner:user)
2. Running container:
   • Name: pg-optimize  
   • Image: postgres:15  
   • Exposed port: 5432 inside container only (not relevant for task)  
   • Environment: POSTGRES_USER=postgres, POSTGRES_DB=shop  
3. Inside the container the database “shop” contains table “orders” with
   enough rows to force a sequential scan.

Grading expectations
--------------------
After the agent finishes, the grader will:

1. Read the file  /home/user/query_plans/orders_full_scan.log  
2. Assert that:
   a) File exists and is a regular file.  
   b) Its first three non-empty lines are exactly:

      Line 1: “QUERY PLAN”
      Line 2: “-----------”
      (Line 3 or later): a line that includes the literal substring
      “Seq Scan on orders”

3. Confirm that the file ends with a single newline and contains no
   extra blank lines at the start.

No further side-effects (new files, containers, processes) will be
checked.
